rationale.root_cause: It correctly describes an unbounded strcpy from a command-line argument into a 256-byte stack buffer causing a stack-buffer overflow, but it does not identify the unsafe copy at the required line 10 and even references argv[1] instead of the actual argc[1]. The rubric requires pinpointing the exact bug line.
rationale.local_fix: It replaces strcpy with strncpy and forces NUL termination, but it does not reject overlong input before copying as required. It also uses argv[1] instead of argc[1].
rationale.global_fix: It recommends removing raw, unbounded string handling by validating argv length up front and using bounded string APIs (e.g., strlcpy/snprintf) or eliminating fixed-size buffers, which satisfies the root-cause mitigation requirement. These practices centralize validation and carry capacities, addressing the broader class of bugs.
